message.content:
You are inventorying a React/TypeScript app's references to a shared Supabase Postgres schema that just changed, so a developer with NO context can mechanically update this app. Be VERY thorough and report exact file paths and line numbers with code snippets.

Repo to inspect: C:\repos\.tmp-apps\popcrm-web  (this is the CRM app "popcrm-web")
IMPORTANT: IGNORE the vendored folder `shared-db/` entirely (it's an auto-synced read-only copy) and ignore `node_modules`. Only inspect the app's own code (mostly under `src/`).

THE SCHEMA CHANGE (what was done in the shared DB):
1. Table `core.company` was HARD-RENAMED to `core.customer` (no compatibility view — `core.company` no longer exists at all). Satellite table `core.company_source_ref` and all `company_id` FK columns KEPT their names.
2. New boolean column `core.customer.is_potential` (true = potential customer not yet in PLM/ERP; false = active/confirmed customer that has a PLM/ERP source ref). A trigger keeps it authoritative.
3. Email-domain noise no longer lives in the customer table. New CRM-private table `crm.ingested_domain` is the front door. The email/Fireflies worker must call RPC `crm.record_ingested_domain(p_domain, p_sender, p_subject, p_display_name)` instead of inserting a company. A human "promotes" a domain via RPC `crm.promote_ingested_domain(p_domain, p_name)` which creates a POTENTIAL customer. New browser view `api.crm_ingested_domain_list` lists them.
4. `api.crm_account_list` now also exposes an `is_potential` column. RPC `api.crm_update_account(...)` now returns a `core.customer` row (same columns as before, plus is_potential).
5. The previous notion of "New Companies"/UNASSIGNED accounts in the Accounts→Triage tab should now be backed by `crm.ingested_domain` (via `api.crm_ingested_domain_list`), not by core.customer rows with customer_status=UNASSIGNED.

WHAT I NEED YOU TO FIND AND REPORT (with file:line + snippet for every hit):
A. Every reference to the string `core.company`, `company` as a Supabase table name (e.g. `.from('company')`, `.from("company")`, `.rpc(...)`), or `company`/`Company` types coming from generated Supabase types.
B. The generated Supabase types file (likely `src/lib/database.types.ts`): does it contain `core.company` / a `company` table type? (It will need regeneration.) Report its path and how types are generated (look for a script in package.json like `gen:types`, `supabase gen types`, any project-ref).
C. Everywhere the app reads/writes accounts/customers: which `api.crm_*` views and which RPCs (`crm_update_account`, etc.). List each call site.
D. The Accounts page and its tabs (Customers / Triage / "New Companies" / Not a customer / All) — where customer_status is filtered/displayed, and where "New Companies"/UNASSIGNED is handled. Files like AccountsPage, AccountDrawer, queries.ts, api.ts, constants.ts.
E. Anything dealing with `customer_status`, `is_potential`, chain_type, routing_aliases, so_patterns.
F. Is there an email-ingestion / Fireflies / routing worker IN THIS REPO that creates company rows? (There may be a separate worker repo "popcrm-fireflies" — if you see references to it, note that, but report any in-repo code that inserts/upserts companies or ingested domains.)
G. The TanStack Query hooks/keys (queries.ts) related to accounts/companies that will need new query keys for the ingested-domain list.
H. README / docs / architecture.md in the app (NOT in shared-db/) that describe the data model and would need updating.

Output a structured inventory grouped by A–H, each item as `path:line` + a one-line snippet + a one-line note on what change it implies. Do not modify any files. Be exhaustive — missing a reference means the app breaks.

Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. - anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. - anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". - code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

1	import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase'
2	import type {
3	  CrmAiModelConfig,
4	  Buyer,
5	  CrmDepartment,
6	  CrmEmailMessage,
7	  CrmIgnoreRule,
8	  CrmLicensorApprovalThread,
9	  CrmMeetingNote,
10	  CrmNote,
11	  CrmOpportunity,
12	  CrmTask,
13	  Retailer,
14	} from '@/lib/types'
15	
16	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
17	// CRM data access over the shared Supabase backend (see u2giants/shared-db).
18	//
19	//   • Reads go through the browser-safe `api.crm_*` views (security_invoker,
20	//     RLS-enforced, no raw email bodies / transcripts / ingest payloads).
21	//   • crm.* operational writes go directly to the `crm` schema tables (baseline
22	//     crm_write RLS lets sales/licensing/admin write them).
23	//   • Canonical company/contact (account/contact) writes go through the guarded
24	//     api.crm_update_account / api.crm_update_contact RPCs.
25	//
26	// View rows are flat (company_id + company_name, ...); adapters below rebuild the
27	// nested {id, name} shapes the existing UI/types expect, so pages stay unchanged.
28	// "Curated customers" (operational pickers) vs "ingested registry" (triage) is one
29	// core.company table split by customer_status — see fetchRetailers/fetchIngestedDomains.
30	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
31	
32	const CUSTOMER_STATUSES = ['ACTIVE_CUSTOMER', 'POTENTIAL_CUSTOMER']
33	
34	type Row = Record<string, unknown>
35	type Order = { col: string; asc?: boolean }
36	export type AccountSegment = 'active' | 'triage' | 'dismissed' | 'all'
37	export type AccountSegmentCounts = Record<AccountSegment, number>
38	export type ContactSegment = 'customer' | 'department' | 'triage' | 'all'
39	export type ContactSegmentCounts = Record<ContactSegment, number>
40	export interface CrmSearchResults {
41	  accounts: Retailer[]
42	  contacts: Buyer[]
43	  opportunities: CrmOpportunity[]
44	  emails: CrmEmailMessage[]
45	}
46	
47	// Dynamic table/view names and generic Row payloads can't satisfy the generated
48	// literal-keyed client types, so the data helpers below go through this untyped
49	// surface. RPC calls (updateRetailer/updateBuyer) stay on the typed client so
50	// their argument names remain checked against database.types.ts.
51	// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
52	const anyDb = supabase as any
53	
54	// Build a nested relation object ({id, name, ...extra}) or null when no id.
55	function rel(id: unknown, name: unknown, extra: Row = {}): Row | null {
56	  return id ? { id: id as string, name: (name ?? null) as string | null, ...extra } : null
57	}
58	
59	// A relation value from the UI may arrive as an id string, a nested object, or null.
60	function relId(value: unknown): string | null {
61	  if (!value) return null
62	  if (typeof value === 'string') return value
63	  if (typeof value === 'object' && 'id' in (value as Row)) return ((value as Row).id as string) ?? null
64	  return null
65	}
66	
67	// Page through an api view so we match the old Directus `limit: -1` (fetch all).
68	async function fetchAllRows(view: string, order: Order[] = []): Promise<Row[]> {
69	  const PAGE = 1000
70	  const out: Row[] = []
71	  let from = 0
72	  for (;;) {
73	    let q = anyDb.schema("api").from(view).select('*').range(from, from + PAGE - 1)
74	    for (const o of order) q = q.order(o.col, { ascending: o.asc ?? true, nullsFirst: false })
75	    const { data, error } = await q
76	    if (error) throw error
77	    const rows = (data ?? []) as Row[]
78	    out.push(...rows)
79	    if (rows.length < PAGE) break
80	    from += PAGE
81	  }
82	  return out
83	}
84	
85	async function fetchRows(view: string, order: Order[] = [], limit?: number): Promise<Row[]> {
86	  if (limit === undefined || limit < 0) return fetchAllRows(view, order)
87	  let q = anyDb.schema("api").from(view).select('*').limit(limit)
88	  for (const o of order) q = q.order(o.col, { ascending: o.asc ?? true, nullsFirst: false })
89	  const { data, error } = await q
90	  if (error) throw error
91	  return (data ?? []) as Row[]
92	}
93	
94	async function fetchRowsEq(view: string, column: string, value: string, order: Order[] = [], limit?: number): Promise<Row[]> {
95	  const PAGE = 1000
96	  const out: Row[] = []
97	  let from = 0
98	  for (;;) {
99	    const cappedEnd = limit !== undefined && limit >= 0 ? Math.min(from + PAGE - 1, limit - 1) : from + PAGE - 1
100	    let q = anyDb.schema("api").from(view).select('*').eq(column, value).range(from, cappedEnd)

127	function applyAccountSegment(q: ReturnType<typeof anyDb.schema>, segment: AccountSegment) {
128	  if (segment === 'active') return q.in('customer_status', CUSTOMER_STATUSES)
129	  if (segment === 'triage') return q.or('customer_status.eq.UNASSIGNED,customer_status.is.null')
130	  if (segment === 'dismissed') return q.eq('customer_status', 'OTHER')
131	  return q
132	}
133	
134	async function fetchAccountSegmentRows(segment: AccountSegment, limit = -1): Promise<Row[]> {
135	  if (segment === 'all') return fetchRows('crm_account_list', [{ col: 'name' }], limit)
136	  const PAGE = 1000
137	  const out: Row[] = []
138	  let from = 0
139	  for (;;) {
140	    const cappedEnd = limit >= 0 ? Math.min(from + PAGE - 1, limit - 1) : from + PAGE - 1
141	    const q = applyAccountSegment(
142	      anyDb.schema("api").from('crm_account_list').select('*').order('name').range(from, cappedEnd),
143	      segment,
144	    )
145	    const { data, error } = await q
146	    if (error) throw error
147	    const rows = (data ?? []) as Row[]
148	    out.push(...rows)
149	    if (rows.length < PAGE || (limit >= 0 && out.length >= limit)) break
150	    from += PAGE
151	  }
152	  return limit >= 0 ? out.slice(0, limit) : out
153	}

1	// Domain constants and status→presentation mappings for the CRM.
2	// Keep these token-driven (no ad-hoc Tailwind colors): badge variants and
3	// stage chip classes map to the shared design tokens in src/index.css.

51	// `OTHER` means "Other" for chain_type/contact_type, but "Not a Customer" here.
52	// `UNASSIGNED`, empty and null all collapse to the single "New Company" bucket.
53	export const CUSTOMER_STATUS_LABEL: Record<string, string> = {
54	  ACTIVE_CUSTOMER: 'Active Customer',
55	  POTENTIAL_CUSTOMER: 'Potential Customer',
56	  OTHER: 'Not a Customer',

60	export function customerStatusLabel(status: string | null | undefined): string {
61	  if (!status) return 'New Company'
62	  return CUSTOMER_STATUS_LABEL[status] ?? label(status)
63	}
64	
65	// Account status → badge tone. Green = active customer, yellow = potential,
66	// blue = New Company (untriaged, needs review), gray = Not a Customer
67	// (reviewed, no CRM action needed). No red — none of these is an alert.
68	export function customerStatusTone(status: string | null | undefined): StatusTone {
69	  switch (status) {
70	    case 'ACTIVE_CUSTOMER':
71	      return 'success'
72	    case 'POTENTIAL_CUSTOMER':
73	      return 'warning'
74	    case 'OTHER':
75	      return 'neutral'
76	    default:
77	      // UNASSIGNED / empty / null = New Company
78	      return 'info'
79	  }
80	}
81

83	export const CHAIN_HUE: Record<string, number> = {
84	  OFF_PRICE: 25,

116	// A message still needs human routing unless it is fully ROUTED or SKIPPED.
117	export function needsRouting(status: string | null | undefined) {
118	  return !!status && status !== 'ROUTED' && status !== 'SKIPPED'
119	}
120	
121	// Map a pipeline stage to the token-backed stage-chip palette (see index.css).
122	export function stageChipClass(stage: string | null | undefined): string {
123	  switch (stage) {
124	    case 'DIRECTIVE_RECEIVED':
125	      return 'bg-stage-dev text-stage-dev-fg'
126	    case 'DESIGN_IN_PROGRESS':
127	      return 'bg-stage-concept text-stage-concept-fg'
128	    case 'BUYER_REVIEW':
129	      return 'bg-stage-review text-stage-review-fg'
130	    case 'PRICING_AND_SAMPLING':
131	      return 'bg-stage-onhold text-stage-onhold-fg'
132	    case 'AWAITING_SALES_ORDER':
133	      return 'bg-stage-production text-stage-production-fg'
134	    case 'IN_PRODUCTION':
135	      return 'bg-stage-approved text-stage-approved-fg'
136	    case 'SHIPPED':
137	      return 'bg-stage-shipped text-stage-shipped-fg'
138	    case 'CLOSED':
139	      return 'bg-muted text-muted-foreground'
140	    default:
141	      return 'bg-muted text-muted-foreground'
142	  }
143	}
144	
145	export type StatusTone = 'neutral' | 'info' | 'success' | 'warning' | 'danger'
146	
147	// Generic tone for a status string, used by CrmStatusBadge. Tone → token classes
148	// live in CrmStatusBadge so color stays centralized.
149	export function routingTone(status: string | null | undefined): StatusTone {
150	  switch (status) {
151	    case 'ROUTED':
152	      return 'success'
153	    case 'SKIPPED':
154	      return 'neutral'
155	    case 'COMPANY_ONLY':
156	    case 'COMPANY_DEPT':
157	      return 'info'
158	    case 'UNROUTED':
159	    case 'CUSTOMER_EMAIL_NO_COMPANY':
160	      return 'danger'
161	    default:
162	      return 'warning'
163	  }
164	}
165	
166	export function taskTone(status: string | null | undefined): StatusTone {
167	  switch (status) {
168	    case 'DONE':
169	      return 'success'
170	    case 'IN_PROGRESS':
171	      return 'info'
172	    case 'CANCELED':
173	      return 'neutral'
174	    default:
175	      return 'warning'
176	  }
177	}
178	
179	// The approval `stage` is a free-form string in Directus (no fixed enum), so
180	// tone is matched on keywords rather than exact values.
181	export function approvalTone(stage: string | null | undefined): StatusTone {
182	  const s = (stage ?? '').toLowerCase()
183	  if (/(approv|complete|signed)/.test(s)) return 'success'
184	  if (/(reject|declin|denied)/.test(s)) return 'danger'
185	  if (/(revis|hold|change)/.test(s)) return 'warning'
186	  if (/(submit|review|pending|sent)/.test(s)) return 'info'
187	  return 'neutral'
188	}
189	
190	// An approval is "resolved" once its stage reads as approved/rejected/closed.
191	export function isApprovalResolved(stage: string | null | undefined): boolean {
192	  const s = (stage ?? '').toLowerCase()
193	  return /(approv|reject|declin|denied|complete|closed|signed)/.test(s)
194	}

201	] as const

1	# AGENTS.md — popcrm-web
2	
3	Canonical operating guide and documentation router for **popcrm-web**. Read this
4	first; load other docs only when the task needs them (see the Documentation map).
5	
6	## Project summary
7	
8	`popcrm-web` is the **POP CRM frontend** — a Vite/React/TypeScript single-page app
9	served by nginx, used by internal POP Creations staff to run customer, sales,
10	licensing and product-development workflows: accounts, contacts, the program
11	pipeline, Outlook-ingested email routing, Fireflies meeting notes, notes, tasks,
12	licensor approvals, and AI-model settings.
13	
14	It **stores no data of its own** — every read/write goes through the shared
15	Supabase project (`https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co`). The CRM backend is
16	a Supabase/Postgres port of the earlier Twenty→Directus CRM data model. The
17	outcome that matters: a fast, dense operations console over that shared data.
18	
19	- Production: `https://crm.designflow.app`
20	- Preview alias: `https://crm-dev.designflow.app`
21	- Backend (Supabase): `https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co`
22	- Fireflies webhook/health: `https://crm-fireflies.designflow.app`
23	- Sibling frontends (separate repos): `poppim-web` (PIM), `popdam-web` (DAM)
24	
25	## Multi-model AI note
26	
27	There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.
28	
29	`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.
30	
31	When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.
32	
33	## Documentation map: what to read for each task
34	
35	Always start with:
36	
37	- `AGENTS.md`
38	
39	Then load additional docs only when relevant:
40	
41	| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
42	|---|---|---|
43	| Quick repo orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | Deep docs under `docs/`, `frontend_imp.md` |
44	| Modify app behavior or project-owned code | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md` if data flow/shape changes | `docs/deployment.md` unless deploy behavior changes |
45	| Add/change config, env vars, or runtime settings | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, `docs/deployment.md` if prod/runtime is affected | Unrelated architecture docs |
46	| Change local setup, dev/test/lint scripts, or tooling | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/development.md`, `package.json`, `eslint.config.js` | `docs/deployment.md` unless CI/CD changes |
47	| Change deployment, Docker, CI/CD, hosting, release, or rollback | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/deployment.md`, `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`, `Dockerfile`, `nginx.conf` | Local-only dev docs |
48	| Change data shape, Supabase fields, queries, views, RPCs, or identifiers | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `src/lib/types.ts`, `src/lib/database.types.ts`, `src/features/crm/api.ts`, `shared-db/supabase/migrations/` | Deployment docs unless rollout changes |
49	| Investigate bugs or incidents | `AGENTS.md` (Critical incidents), area-relevant source, `HANDOFF.md` if present | Unrelated docs |
50	| Continue unfinished work | `AGENTS.md`, `HANDOFF.md` (if present) and the docs it names | Docs outside the handoff scope |
51	| Understand the redesign rationale (charts, tokens, layout) | `frontend_imp.md` (historical plan — fully implemented) | Everything else |
52	| Claude Code session | `CLAUDE.md`, then `AGENTS.md` | Other docs unless the task requires them |
53	| Documentation-only cleanup | `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, affected `docs/`, ignore files | Source files except to verify accuracy |
54	| Pull secrets from 1Password (MCP server or `op` CLI) | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/1password.md` | Unrelated architecture/deploy docs |
55	
56	Notes:
57	- `HANDOFF.md` is **absent** when there is no unfinished work. If it exists, it is
58	  required reading for continuation tasks.
59	- `frontend_imp.md` is a large background design plan — now fully implemented. It is in
60	  `.claudeignore`/`.cursorignore` to keep it out of routine AI context.
61	- `design_handoff_popcrm_elevation/` is a historical design-spec directory — also ignored.
62	- Shared non-code infrastructure/server standards live in
63	  [`u2giants/albert-standards/infrastructure`](https://github.com/u2giants/albert-standards/tree/main/infrastructure).
64	  When deployment, domains, runtime ownership, server dependencies, break-glass
65	  runbooks, or infrastructure decisions change for this app, update that repo too.
66	
67	## Shared-backend startup/shutdown hygiene
68	
69	Why this exists:
70	`popcrm-web`, `poppim-web`, and `popdam3` all depend on the same Supabase backend.
71	An unfinished migration or dirty canonical `u2giants/shared-db` checkout can block
72	unrelated app commits or, worse, ship a database change without the right preview
73	checks. Future AI sessions must keep shared-db work isolated and leave the
74	workspace clean enough for the next vibe-coding session.
75	
76	Startup checklist:
77	
78	1. Run `git status --short` in this repo before editing.
79	2. If the task may touch Supabase schema, RLS, API views/RPCs, generated database
80	   types, or cross-app data contracts, also run `git status --short` in
81	   `/worksp/shared-db` before editing.
82	3. Treat `shared-db/` inside this repo as a read-only mirror. Do not create or
83	   edit migrations there; use canonical `/worksp/shared-db`.
84	4. If `/worksp/shared-db` has untracked migrations or unrelated dirty files, stop
85	   and report them before creating new database work. Do not mix another
86	   session's shared-db changes into this app's commit.
87	5. Before creating a shared-db migration, create/switch to a dedicated
88	   `/worksp/shared-db` branch named for the database change. App repos commit to
89	   `main`; shared-db uses branch + PR.
90	
91	Shutdown checklist:
92	
93	1. Run `git status --short` in this repo and, if touched or inspected for backend
94	   work, in `/worksp/shared-db`.
95	2. No untracked shared-db migration may remain. Every shared-db migration must be
96	   committed on its own branch, stashed with a clear name, or removed if
97	   abandoned.
98	3. If shared-db work is incomplete, leave durable handoff text that names the
99	   branch/stash, migration file, preview/prod apply status, and the next exact
100	   action.
101	4. Final reports must separate app commits from shared-db status so the owner can
102	   keep vibe-coding without becoming the git janitor.
103	
104	## Repository structure
105	
106	Project-owned application code:
107	
108	- `src/app/` — shell + router: `AppLayout.tsx`, `routes.tsx`, `navigation.ts`
109	- `src/components/app/` — shared building blocks: `DataTable`, `DetailDrawer`,
110	  `MetricCard`, `PageToolbar`, `AppPage`, `AppSidebar`, `AppHeader`, `Combobox`,
111	  `CommandSearch`, `FilterSelect`, `StatusBadge`, `states.tsx`
112	- `src/features/crm/` — CRM domain:
113	  - `CrmDataContext.tsx` — loads all collections once; exposes state, refresh, stats
114	  - `api.ts` — Supabase view/RPC/table reads and writes · `constants.ts` · `format.ts` · `useRecordSelection.ts`
115	  - `pages/` — one module per route (Overview, Pipeline, Accounts, Contacts,
116	    EmailRouting, Meetings, Notes, Tasks, Approvals, Settings) + `_shared.ts`
117	  - `components/` — domain drawers (Email/Opportunity/Account/Contact/Task/Note/
118	    Meeting/Approval) + `CrmStatusBadge`, `RelationLabel`
119	- `src/auth/auth.tsx` — Supabase auth/profile state · `src/pages/LoginPage.tsx`
120	- `src/lib/` — `supabase.ts` (client), `database.types.ts` (generated schema), `types.ts` (frontend domain types), `utils.ts`
121	- `src/App.tsx`, `src/main.tsx`, `src/index.css` (shared OKLCH design tokens)
122	
123	Generated-style primitives (shadcn): `src/components/ui/*` — see Core modification inventory.
124	
125	Build / config / deploy:
126	
127	- `Dockerfile`, `nginx.conf`, `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
128	- `vite.config.ts`, `tsconfig*.json`, `eslint.config.js`, `components.json`, `package.json`
129	
130	Docs: `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/*`, `frontend_imp.md`
131	
132	Build artifacts / ignored: `dist/`, `node_modules/` (see What to ignore).
133	
134	Shared Supabase migrations live under `shared-db/supabase/migrations/`; legacy
135	Directus code under `/worksp/directus` is rollback/reference context only.
136	
137	## Prime Directive: custom-code boundary
138	
139	Our custom code lives here:
140	
141	- `src/app/`
142	- `src/components/app/`
143	- `src/features/`
144	- `src/auth/`, `src/pages/`, `src/lib/`
145	- `docs/`
146	- `.github/workflows/`
147	- root config: `Dockerfile`, `nginx.conf`, `vite.config.ts`, `tsconfig*.json`, `eslint.config.js`, `package.json`
148	
149	Everything else requires justification before touching. In particular,
150	`src/components/ui/*` are shadcn-style primitives (see below).
151	
152	## Core modification inventory
153	
154	Files modified outside the clearly project-owned application areas:
155	
156	| File | Change made | Why it was necessary | Risk during upgrades |
157	|---|---|---|---|
158	| `src/components/ui/*.tsx` | Hand-authored shadcn-style primitives (tabs, select, table, tooltip, popover, dialog, label, chart, etc.) using the unified `radix-ui` import style | shadcn CLI/registry not run here; primitives added to match existing ones | Running `npx shadcn add` could overwrite/conflict; keep the `radix-ui` (not `@radix-ui/*`) import style and Lucide icons |
159	| `Dockerfile` | Added `COMMIT_HASH` / `COMMIT_DATE` / `VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN` build args before `npm run build` | `.git` is dockerignored (commit identity passed by CI); `VITE_*` is build-time so the logo.dev token must be baked at build | If the header build-stamp is removed, drop the commit args too; `VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN` is optional (empty → initials avatars) |
160	
161	No third-party/vendor/framework source files are modified. `src/components/ui` is
162	the only "generated-style" area, and it is hand-maintained in this repo.
163	
164	## Task-to-file navigation: what to edit for common changes
165	
166	| Task | Files to touch | Files not to touch |
167	|---|---|---|

No logo field — see Quirks |

241	## Intentional quirks and non-obvious decisions
242	
243	### Bootstrap loader tolerates a failing collection
244	
245	Looks like:
246	`CrmDataContext.load()` uses `Promise.allSettled` and per-collection setters instead of one `Promise.all`.
247	
248	Actually:
249	Each Supabase view/table/RPC load runs independently; a failing one leaves its
250	section empty and a hard error shows only if everything fails.
251	
252	Why:
253	A single 403 (e.g. a Directus permission/schema gap on one collection) previously blanked the entire app. See Critical incidents 2026-06-12.
254	
255	Do not change because:
256	Reverting to all-or-nothing makes one bad collection take down every page.
257	
258	### Commit stamp comes from build args, not git, in CI
259	
260	Looks like:
261	`vite.config.ts` reads `process.env.COMMIT_HASH` / `COMMIT_DATE` before falling back to `git`.
262	
263	Actually:
264	The Docker build context excludes `.git` (`.dockerignore`), so git is unavailable inside the image build; CI passes the values as Docker build args.
265	
266	Why:
267	The app header shows the deployed commit + NYC time; without build args the stamp would be empty in CI builds.
268	
269	Do not change because:
270	Removing the env path silently blanks the build stamp in production.
271	
272	### `crm_licensor_approval_thread.stage` is free-form
273	
274	Looks like:
275	There's no `APPROVAL_STATUSES` enum; tone/labels are keyword-matched (`approvalTone`, `isApprovalResolved` in `constants.ts`).
276	
277	Actually:
278	The CRM approval `stage` field has no fixed choices, and the collection currently
279	has 0 rows.
280	
281	Why:
282	The real schema differs from earlier assumptions; matching keywords is robust to unknown values.
283	
284	Do not change because:
285	Hard-coding an enum would re-introduce the wrong field/value assumptions that caused the 2026-06-12 incident. Verify real values via the backend once approval data exists.
286	
287	### `customer_status` labels/tones are context-specific — `OTHER` is NOT a global label override
288	
289	Looks like:
290	You could add `OTHER: 'Not a Customer'` to `format.ts` `LABEL_OVERRIDES` to relabel it.
291	
292	Actually:
293	`OTHER` is a shared enum value — it also means "Other" for `chain_type`, `contact_type`,
294	and routing. The account-status relabel/recolor lives in
295	`constants.ts:customerStatusLabel` / `customerStatusTone` (and `CUSTOMER_STATUS_LABEL`),
296	used only by the Accounts table/drawer — never in the global `label()`.
297	
298	Why:
299	A global `OTHER` override would wrongly rename every other column's "Other".

1	# Architecture — popcrm-web
2	
3	`popcrm-web` is a static React SPA for POP CRM.
4	
5	```txt
6	Browser
7	  -> https://crm.designflow.app
8	  -> popcrm-web nginx container
9	  -> React/Vite SPA
10	  -> Supabase JS client
11	  -> https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co
12	  -> shared Supabase/Postgres backend
13	```
14	
15	Fireflies integration is handled by a separate worker endpoint:
16	
17	```txt
18	Fireflies
19	  -> https://crm-fireflies.designflow.app/s/fireflies-webhook
20	  -> popcrm-fireflies container
21	  -> Supabase CRM tables
22	```
23	
24	Important operational note: the Fireflies `/health` endpoint only proves the
25	webhook server is up. It does not prove Fireflies is delivering webhook events.
26	On 2026-06-14, the CRM backend had 27 `crm_meeting_note` rows with latest meeting
27	date `2026-04-14`, while the Fireflies API listed newer transcripts through
28	`2026-06-11` and worker/proxy logs showed no recent webhook deliveries. Verify
29	stale ingestion from CRM row dates, worker/proxy logs, and the Fireflies
30	dashboard webhook configuration.
31	
32	The frontend has no database and stores no CRM data locally. It uses Supabase
33	Auth in the browser, then loads the user's CRM profile with
34	`api.current_user_profile()`.
35	
36	## Data loading
37	
38	Data loading is page-scoped through TanStack Query hooks in
39	`src/features/crm/queries.ts`. The app no longer bootstraps every CRM collection
40	on mount. Until a screen has real server-side pagination/search/filtering plus
41	true aggregate counts, list queries must load the full relevant dataset through
42	the paged helpers in `api.ts`. A 2026-06-22 TanStack Query refactor briefly used
43	hard-coded 50/100/300 row limits and made production screens look empty even
44	though data was intact. Page-scoped is fine; arbitrary partial slices are not.
45	High-change surfaces such as Email Routing, Tasks, Meetings, and Notes also use
46	conservative background refetch intervals.
47	
48	Reads in `src/features/crm/api.ts` go through browser-safe `api.crm_*` views.
49	Core account/contact writes use guarded RPCs (`api.crm_update_account`,
50	`api.crm_update_contact`); CRM-owned rows write to `crm.*` tables or narrower RPCs.
51	Write operations use mutation hooks in `queries.ts`, with optimistic query-cache
52	updates where the UI already had instant behavior. Related queries are invalidated
53	after writes so joined view rows reconcile with Supabase.
54	
55	Contact writes have one extra wrinkle: CRM relationship attributes
56	(`department`, `contact_type`, `scope`) live on `core.contact_company`, not
57	`core.contact`. The frontend must send the current account when editing those
58	fields so `api.crm_update_contact` can target the correct relationship row. The
59	2026-06-23 migration
60	`shared-db/supabase/migrations/20260623024500_crm_update_contact_clear_relationship_fields.sql`
61	adds explicit `p_clear_*` flags because `null` alone is ambiguous in RPC calls
62	and the old `coalesce` semantics could not clear values. Until that migration is
63	confirmed applied everywhere, `updateBuyer` keeps a fallback call using the old
64	RPC argument shape.
65	
66	Realtime frontend scaffolding lives in `src/features/crm/realtime.ts`. It listens
67	for base-table changes, debounces bursty tables, and invalidates the relevant
68	query keys so the app refetches browser-safe `api.crm_*` views instead of trying
69	to render raw realtime payloads. Supabase publication/RLS enablement is shared-db
70	work and must follow `shared-db/AGENTS.md`.
71	
72	Contacts are intentionally special after the 2026-06-22 incidents. The frontend
73	uses `api.crm_contact_segment_list` for the Cust Contacts, Dept. Contacts, and
74	Triage tabs, and `api.crm_contact_segment_counts` for tab counts. The All tab
75	uses the same segmented view without a segment filter, but it is lazy-loaded only
76	after the user opens All. Do not reintroduce PostgREST `order('name')` or
77	server-side `company_customer_status` filters for contact reads: the previous
78	`security_invoker` view plus derived-field ordering/filtering timed out on the
79	third paged browser request and made Contacts show all zeroes. The durable
80	database fix is
81	`shared-db/supabase/migrations/20260622043000_crm_contact_segments.sql`, which
82	preserves `api.crm_contact_list`, adds the explicit `app.has_app_access('crm')`
83	guard, and exposes the server-computed segment/count contracts.
84	
85	## Key modules
86	
87	| Module | Purpose |

89	| `src/auth/auth.tsx` | Supabase Auth session plus CRM profile state |
90	| `src/lib/supabase.ts` | Supabase JS client and Microsoft/Azure OAuth helper |

94	| `src/features/crm/queries.ts` | TanStack Query keys, full/segmented read hooks, mutation hooks, Fireflies health query |

99	| `src/app/AppLayout.tsx` + `src/app/routes.tsx` | App shell and route-per-page |
100	
101	## Page components (`src/features/crm/pages/`)

Segmented tabs: **Customers** (default — only curated customers; hides both "Not a Customer" `OTHER` **and** untriaged New Companies `UNASSIGNED`), **Triage** (New Companies awaiting review), **Not a customer**, **All**. The tab was renamed from "Accounts" to "Customers" (2026-06-21) to disambiguate it from the page title, and New Companies were moved out of it into Triage only. Status & Chain cells are inline-editable colored chips |

Contacts** (linked to Active/Potential account and department), **Triage** (not linked to a customer account), **All**. Account inline-edit choices are row-aware |
111	| `EmailRoutingPage` | `/email` | Segmented tabs (company/dept/program/triage/admin-only all); ignore-rules sidebar |

118	## Shared app components (`src/components/app/`)

124	| `DataTable` | Sortable table with column visibility, resize (visible separator), reorder, and optional `groupBy` row group headers. Per-column tools in the header: a persistent filter icon → checkbox **value popover** (set filter), and a quick-**search box with value autocomplete**. Optional inline editing: columns with `editOptions` render click-to-edit dropdowns and a spreadsheet **drag-to-copy** fill handle; `editOptions` may be a static array or row-aware function. Edits persist via the `onCellEdit` prop. Columns with `opensDetail` are the only detail-drawer triggers once any column opts in. Popovers/autocomplete use fixed positioning to escape `overflow:hidden` |

1	// Display formatting helpers shared across CRM screens.
2	
3	// Overrides for values whose generic snake→title conversion is wrong or ugly.
4	const LABEL_OVERRIDES: Record<string, string> = {
5	  AI: 'AI',
6	  DETERMINISTIC: 'Rule match',
7	  COMPANY_ONLY: 'Company only',
8	  COMPANY_DEPT: 'Company + dept',
9	  COMPANY_EMAIL_NO_COMPANY: 'Company (no match)',
10	  CUSTOMER_EMAIL_NO_COMPANY: 'Unmatched sender',
11	  IN_PROGRESS: 'In progress',
12	  TODO: 'To do',
13	  // Account status / chain — match the real Directus schema choice labels.
14	  UNASSIGNED: 'New Company',
15	  OFF_PRICE: 'Off-Price',
16	  ECOM: 'eCom',
17	  GROCERY_DRUG: 'Grocery/Drug',
18	}
19	
20	// Turn an UPPER_SNAKE enum or snake field name into a readable label.
21	export function label(value: string | null | undefined) {
22	  if (!value) return 'Unspecified'
23	  if (LABEL_OVERRIDES[value]) return LABEL_OVERRIDES[value]
24	  return value
25	    .toLowerCase()
26	    .split('_')
27	    .map((part) => (part[0]?.toUpperCase() ?? '') + part.slice(1))
28	    .join(' ')
29	}
30	
31	// Resolve the display name of a Directus relation that may be expanded or an id.
32	// Handles named records (name/title) and user records (first/last/email).
33	export function relatedName(
34	  value:
35	    | string
36	    | {
37	        name?: string | null
38	        title?: string | null
39	        first_name?: string | null
40	        last_name?: string | null
41	        email?: string | null
42	      }
43	    | null
44	    | undefined,
45	) {
46	  if (!value) return '—'
47	  if (typeof value === 'string') return '—'
48	  if (value.name) return value.name
49	  if (value.title) return value.title
50	  const full = [value.first_name, value.last_name].filter(Boolean).join(' ')
51	  if (full) return full
52	  return value.email || '—'
53	}
54	
55	// Resolve the id of a relation that may be expanded or an id string.
56	export function idOf(value: string | { id?: string } | null | undefined) {
57	  if (!value) return ''
58	  return typeof value === 'string' ? value : value.id || ''
59	}
60	
61	export function textOf(...values: unknown[]) {
62	  return values.filter(Boolean).join(' ').toLowerCase()
63	}
64	
65	export function initials(name: string) {
66	  return (
67	    name
68	      .split(' ')
69	      .filter(Boolean)
70	      .slice(0, 2)
71	      .map((s) => s[0]?.toUpperCase())
72	      .join('') || '?'
73	  )
74	}
75	
76	export function formatDate(value: string | null | undefined) {
77	  if (!value) return '—'
78	  const date = new Date(value)
79	  if (Number.isNaN(date.getTime())) return value
80	  return date.toLocaleDateString(undefined, {
81	    year: 'numeric',
82	    month: 'short',
83	    day: 'numeric',
84	  })
85	}
86	
87	export function formatDateTime(value: string | null | undefined) {
88	  if (!value) return '—'
89	  const date = new Date(value)
90	  if (Number.isNaN(date.getTime())) return value
91	  return date.toLocaleString(undefined, {
92	    month: 'short',
93	    day: 'numeric',
94	    hour: 'numeric',
95	    minute: '2-digit',
96	  })
97	}
98	
99	export function monthKey(value: string | null | undefined) {
100	  if (!value) return null
101	  const date = new Date(value)
102	  if (Number.isNaN(date.getTime())) return null
103	  return `${date.getFullYear()}-${String(date.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0')}`
104	}
105	
106	export function monthLabel(key: string) {
107	  const [year, month] = key.split('-').map(Number)
108	  const date = new Date(year, (month ?? 1) - 1, 1)
109	  return date.toLocaleDateString(undefined, { month: 'short', year: '2-digit' })
110	}
